top_stories.title: Energy crisis threatens Philippine office demand—Colliers

top_stories.summary: Rising global energy prices driven by Middle East tensions are posing growing risks to the Philippine office real estate sector, as higher power costs threaten tenant demand, rental growth, and asset values, according to property consultancy Colliers. top_stories.summary: By Heather Caitlin P. top_stories.title: Pag-IBIG allots P6.3 billion for SUC rental housing
top_stories.summary: Pag-IBIG Fund has earmarked P6.3 billion to support the development of dormitories and rental housing facilities for its partnership with the Commission on Higher Education (CHED) to expand access to affordable rental housing in state universities and colleges (SUCs) nationwide.

top_stories.title: Property prices record slowest increase since Q1 2019
top_stories.summary: PDate: 3/27/2026 Content: ​ Residential property prices in the Philippines rose by 1.6 percent year-on-year (y-o-y) in Q4 2025, easing from 1.9 percent in Q3 2025.
